meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Expert Workflow for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with UI/UX. Use this intermediate guide to design professional SaaS interfaces using Antigravity and Claude Code for faster client delivery.
content.answer_block: You don't need a degree in design to ship professional SaaS products. By leveraging AI-driven design systems in Antigravity and automating component generation with Claude Code, you can translate raw business requirements into high-fidelity, responsive UIs in hours, not weeks. This workflow eliminates design bottlenecks and accelerates your development velocity.
content.intro:
The blank canvas is a freelancer’s greatest enemy. You have the backend logic and the database schema ready, but the UI sits empty—a stark reminder of your limited design chops. Every hour spent wrestling with CSS or color palettes is an hour of billable time bleeding away. Clients demand sleek, modern interfaces, and 'developer-style' layouts just don't cut it anymore.

The tide is turning. With the integration of Antigravity’s design-to-code capabilities and Claude Code’s agentic development, you can now generate production-ready UI components that aren't just functional, but beautiful. It’s time to stop apologizing for your design skills and start shipping world-class interfaces by letting AI handle the aesthetics while you focus on the architecture.

steps.title: Define Design Constraints with Antigravity
steps.description: Establish a consistent design system (typography, spacing, color palette) to ensure your AI-generated components remain cohesive across the entire application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modern, minimalist design system for a SaaS dashboard. Include a primary color palette (#2563eb), a neutral gray scale, 8px grid spacing, and modern sans-serif typography constraints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a theme.ts file containing the design tokens generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Component Architecture Generation
steps.description: Break down your UI into atomic components before writing code to prevent spaghetti CSS and ensure reusability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a list of necessary atomic components for a SaaS billing dashboard including a data table, navigation sidebar, and action cards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the React/Tailwind component structure based on the atomic design list provided by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Rapid UI Prototyping
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold the layout structure, ensuring responsive behavior and accessibility standards are met from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a responsive dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS. Use the design tokens defined in step 1. Ensure the sidebar is collapsible on mobile.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the dashboard layout in src/components/Layout.tsx using the generated tokens.'

steps.title: Contextual Component Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map your backend API responses to the UI components, ensuring data-driven visuals are accurate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a component for a summary card that displays dynamic revenue data. Ensure it accepts props for value, label, and trend percentage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create src/components/SummaryCard.tsx and integrate it into the dashboard view.'

steps.title: Visual QA and Polish
steps.description: Run an automated visual review to catch spacing inconsistencies or alignment issues that often plague AI-generated code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current UI for common alignment errors and accessibility violations. Suggest refactors for the hero section.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Perform a code review on the UI components and fix any discovered accessibility or alignment issues.'

pro_tips: Always define your design tokens in a central file before generating components to prevent style drift.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your components immediately after generation to ensure visual states don't break during refactors.
When using Antigravity, provide your existing CSS framework (e.g., Tailwind, Shadcn) in the prompt to ensure output matches your current project structure.
Treat your AI-generated UI code as a draft; use Claude Code to perform a 'cleanup' pass to remove redundant utility classes.
Create a 'Component Library' folder in your repo where Claude Code stores all generated UI elements, keeping your main app logic clean.
content.next_steps: Once your UI is scaffolded, focus on hooking up your authentication layer and database queries. Use Claude Code to 'connect the dots' between your newly polished UI and your existing backend services.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design friction slow down your development cycle. Master the AI-assisted workflow and become the freelancer who ships faster than the competition. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
